preamble: Spirantization of the voiceless aspirated velar stop [kʰ] to the velar fricative [x] is another hallmark of phonetic shifts in Greek and Germanic languages. This transformation exemplifies the general trend of lenition across aspirated series.

examples.note: χ (chi) was initially [kʰ] and transitioned to [x] over time.
PIE *k > *kʰ > [x] in Proto-Germanic (e.g., PIE *krut- > English heart).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
